summary: Frontend-only regression test after dark-mode revert. Landing, login (email/password), dashboard, analyze, history, reports, trending, sources, admin, AI assistant toggle, and logout ALL work with zero page/console errors. CRITICAL: /settings page crashes with 'ReferenceError: theme is not defined' — the dark-mode revert was INCOMPLETE. Settings.jsx still contains a Theme <Select value={theme} onValueChange={setTheme}> block referencing undefined variables.

ui_bugs.issue: Uncaught ReferenceError: theme is not defined. Lines 51-61 still contain a Theme <Select value={theme} onValueChange={setTheme}> block that references `theme` and `setTheme` which no longer exist (useTheme() hook and its import were removed but the JSX using them was NOT removed). The whole Settings page fails to render — user sees only the runtime-error overlay. Contrary to the review request claim ('theme select removed, only name + default_model fields'), the theme select is still present in the file.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Settings.jsx delete lines 51-61 (the entire <div> containing <Label>Theme</Label> and the Select for theme). No other file references remain — this is the only leftover from the dark-mode feature.
critical_code_review_comments: Incomplete revert: Settings.jsx JSX block using `theme`/`setTheme` was not removed even though the useTheme import was. Simple grep for 'theme' in /app/frontend/src/pages/ would have caught this before shipping.
Consider a lint rule (no-undef) — the ReferenceError would fail an eslint run and prevent this class of bug.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Only /settings is broken. After main agent deletes the Theme <Select> block (lines 51-61 in Settings.jsx), just re-visit /settings while logged in as test@truthlens.ai/test123 and confirm page renders with Name + Email + Default AI model fields plus Save button. No other page needs re-testing — all other routes verified clean of theme references.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: the dark-mode revert removed the useTheme import at the top of Settings.jsx but forgot to remove the JSX block (lines 51-61) that used `theme` and `setTheme`. React then throws 'ReferenceError: theme is not defined' when rendering Settings, showing the red runtime-error overlay. Reproduction: log in with test@truthlens.ai / test123 → click Settings in the sidebar (or go to /settings) → page fails to render. Mitigation: delete lines 51-61 in /app/frontend/src/pages/Settings.jsx.
